<<Hotel Operations and Probability>>

The housekeeping manager manages 100 rooms in the hotel. Each day 20 percent of rooms need attention. The concierge helps with 40 percent of these concerns.  
* * *  
The Poisson distribution models the number of rooms needing attention. The mean is 20 rooms. The probability of exactly 15 rooms needing attention can be calculated using the Poisson formula.  
* * *  
The concierge resolves 90 percent of the concerns they handle. The binomial distribution calculates the probability of resolving at least 7 out of 8 concerns. The committee [address] the concerns [address] and [address] the effectiveness [address].  

The Poisson probability formula is P(x) = (e^-λ * λ^x) / x! where λ is the mean. For λ = 20 and x = 15 we calculate e^-20 * 20^15 divided by 15 factorial. The result is the probability of exactly 15 rooms needing attention.  

The binomial probability for at least 7 successes out of 8 trials with a 90 percent success rate is calculated by summing the probabilities from 7 to 8. The formula is Σ (from k=7 to 8) (8 choose k) * (0.9)^k * (0.1)^(8-k). The committee [address] the concerns [address] and [address] the effectiveness [address].  

The book [address] is a reference for understanding probability distributions. The book [address] provides examples of Poisson and binomial calculations. The committee [address] the concerns [address] and [address] the effectiveness [address].
